name: There are two reasons why this query is addressed to the Children of Israel. In the first
introduction:
place, a group of living human beings provide a better source of instruction and admonition
than mute archaeological findings. In the second place, the Israelites were a people who,
having been endowed with Scripture and prophethood, were entrusted with the leadership
of the world. By falling prey to worldliness, hypocrisy and other errors of belief and conduct
they subsequently lost God's favour. A people entrusted with the task of universal
leadership could derive no better lesson from another people than this one.
